title: Workshop outcomes and next steps for WP5 BOOST-EDS
author: Townsend, Poppy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This work is part of the BOOST EDS project, funded by UKRI and supported by DSIT, aimed to pilot a range of measures to address issues of data findability, traceability, accessibility and interoperability, to improve data sharing and access across disciplines. Conducted from late 2023 to March 2025, the project consisted of five work packages, with one work package dedicated to user centred approaches in the context of environmental data services. 
 This report summarises the ‘Co-creation of user-focussed services: priorities across the EDS - technical experts workshop’ which was held online on Monday 25th March 2024 10:00am-12:00pm and brought together 24 technical experts from across the Environmental Data Service (and partners). 
 The purpose of the workshop was to understand current (or near future) areas of work being undertaken on user-focussed services across the EDS (and partners), identify barriers to including users in the co-creation of these services, and create a list of potential activities that could be used as case studies for co-creating user-focussed services.
